catalog: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 — Security and Privacy Controls
note: 20 control families. The full catalog (~1000 controls + enhancements) and the authoritative Low/Moderate/High + Privacy baselines are published by NIST in human-readable PDFs and machine-readable OSCAL at https://github.com/usnistgov/oscal-content and https://csrc.nist.gov/projects/risk-management/sp800-53-controls. This file lists every family and a curated set of foundational controls for learning and tooling; pull the complete OSCAL catalog for production use.

families.name: Access Control
families.theme: Who/what may access; least privilege, separation of duties, remote/wireless access, session control

families.name: Awareness and Training
families.theme: Security & privacy literacy, role-based training, records

families.name: Audit and Accountability
families.theme: Event logging, content, storage, review/analysis, time stamps, protection, non-repudiation, retention

families.name: Assessment, Authorization, and Monitoring
families.theme: Control assessments, POA&M, authorization (ATO), continuous monitoring, interconnections, penetration testing

families.name: Configuration Management
families.theme: Baseline config, change control, impact analysis, least functionality, inventory, CM plan

families.name: Contingency Planning
families.theme: Contingency plan, training/testing, backups, alternate sites, recovery/reconstitution

families.name: Identification and Authentication
families.theme: Org/non-org users, MFA, device ID, authenticator management, re-authentication

families.name: Incident Response
families.theme: IR policy, training, testing, handling, monitoring, reporting, response plan, spillage

families.name: Maintenance
families.theme: Controlled/remote maintenance, tools, personnel, timely maintenance

families.name: Media Protection
families.theme: Media access, marking, storage, transport, sanitization, use

families.name: Physical and Environmental Protection
families.theme: Physical access, monitoring, power, fire, temperature, water damage, delivery/removal

families.name: Planning
families.theme: System security & privacy plans (SSP), rules of behavior, security architecture, baseline selection

families.name: Program Management
families.theme: Org-wide program controls (not system-specific): risk mgmt strategy, EA, insider threat, C-SCRM, workforce

families.name: Personnel Security
families.theme: Position risk, screening, termination, transfer, agreements, sanctions

families.name: PII Processing and Transparency
families.theme: Authority/purpose to process PII, consent, privacy notice, minimization, redress

families.name: Risk Assessment
families.theme: Security categorization, risk assessment, vulnerability monitoring/scanning, threat hunting, privacy impact

families.name: System and Services Acquisition
families.theme: SDLC, acquisition process, developer security testing, supply chain, external services

families.name: System and Communications Protection
families.theme: Boundary protection, crypto, denial-of-service, transmission confidentiality/integrity, isolation

families.name: System and Information Integrity
families.theme: Flaw remediation, malware, monitoring, alerts, software/firmware/info integrity, spam

families.name: Supply Chain Risk Management
families.theme: SCRM plan, supplier assessments, provenance, tamper resistance, component authenticity, disposal

baselines.source: FIPS 200 mandates the use of 800-53 controls; SP 800-53B defines the baselines.
privacy: Privacy baseline (applies when system processes PII, independent of impact level)
baselines.selection_rule: Categorize the system (FIPS 199 high-water mark) -> pick the matching security baseline -> tailor (apply overlays, scope, supplement, assign parameters, document) per 800-53B.
